headline: Stryker Stock Falls After Cyberattack
summary: Shares of Stryker retreated after the medical technology company was hit by a cyberattack. The stock fell 3.6% Wednesday. Stryker said that the early morning attack produced a global network disruption to its Microsoft environment, affecting both client devices and servers.

raw.headline: Stryker Stock Falls After Cyberattack

headline: Hims & Hers C-suite shake-up, Stryker outage may be Iran-backed
summary: Yahoo Finance anchor Josh Lipton tracks Wednesday's top moving stocks and biggest market stories in this Market Minute. Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) stock is soaring after the company named Katherine Beir as its next chief communications officer, reporting to CEO Andrew Dudum. Stryker Corporation (SYK) stock is under pressure amid a global outage that could be tied to an Iran-backed hacking group. Stay up to date on the latest market action, minute-by-minute, with Yahoo Finance's Market Minute.

headline: US medical equipment company Stryker says cyberattack disrupted its global networks.
summary: Stryker, a major U.S. medical equipment company, said a cyberattack disrupted its global networks Wednesday.  Stryker's statement said the cyberattack hit its Microsoft programs.  Stryker is based in Portage, Michigan, and makes a variety of medical products, from artificial joints to hospital beds.

raw.headline: US medical equipment company Stryker says cyberattack disrupted its global networks.
raw.summary: Stryker, a major U.S. medical equipment company, said a cyberattack disrupted its global networks Wednesday.  Stryker's statement said the cyberattack hit its Microsoft programs.  Stryker is based in Portage, Michigan, and makes a variety of medical products, from artificial joints to hospital beds.

headline: U.S. Medical Company Hit With Suspected Iran-Linked Cyberattack
summary: Medical technology company Stryker is experiencing a global outage across its systems, with staff and contractors reporting that the logo of an Iran-linked hacking group has appeared on login pages.  Staff found that remote devices running Microsoft’s Windows operating system—such as cellphones, laptops and others configured to connect to Stryker’s technology systems—had been wiped.  A successful attack on a major U.S. company would be a significant escalation in a cyber conflict that had been expected to follow the U.S. and Israel’s military campaign in Iran, which began on Feb. 28.

Staff found that remote devices running Microsoft’s Windows operating system—such as cellphones, laptops and others configured to connect to Stryker’s technology systems—had been wiped.  A successful attack on a major U.S. company would be a significant escalation in a cyber conflict that had been expected to follow the U.S. and Israel’s military campaign in Iran, which began on Feb. 28.

headline: Stryker the target of possible Iran-linked cyberattack
summary: Medical device manufacturer Stryker (SYK) has reported being the target of a cyberattack, according to the Wall Street Journal, which is suspected to have links to Iranian hackers. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Catalysts.

headline: Intuitive Surgical Upgraded to Buy by Citi — Plus Key Moves on Stryker and Abbott
summary: Citi is making a clear statement about the health of U.S. medical technology heading into the spring: the sector’s fundamentals remain intact, even as broader market volatility has dragged down some of the industry’s strongest names. On Wednesday, the firm upgraded Intuitive Surgical (NASDAQ:ISRG) to Buy from Neutral, opened a 90-day catalyst watch on Stryker ...
